stem: A agriculture study measures crop yield across 3 regions, each containing multiple farms. region A has 15 observations with mean 49.8 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 4.6). region B has 5 observations with mean 61.2 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 5.6). region C has 8 observations with mean 53.8 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 9.2). The between-region standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 3.7 and 9.3 tonnes/hectare. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true crop yield for region B, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-region variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for region B: 60.0 tonnes/hectare (shrunk from raw 61.2). Range [58.6, 60.6] as between-region SD varies from 3.7 to 9.3. Pooled mean: 53.0.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 53.0 tonnes/hectare (weighted average of 3 region means).
Shrinkage factor for region B: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/5). At σ_between=6.2: B = 0.86.
Interval [58.6, 60.6] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (61.2) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (53.0) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
